runs.explanation: Lines 80-81 incorrectly remove one duplicate from the merged output when the last version appears in both streams, contradicting the documented behavior that both copies should be kept.
runs.raw:
Looking at the code, the merge logic at lines 72-76 correctly handles duplicates by appending both copies. However, lines 80-81 introduce a bug: after the merge, if the last element of merged equals the last element of both left and right (i.e., the same version appeared at the end of both streams), it removes one copy — contradicting the docstring that says "both copies are kept in the merged output."
